articles.title: Cruise Ship Size, Explained: When Bigger Feels Smaller (and Vice Versa)
articles.excerpt: Gross tonnage is the headline number on every comparison chart. It's also the wrong number for predicting how a ship will feel onboard. The metric that matters is space ratio — and once you understand it, the chart reads very differently. Icon of the Seas at 250,800 GT and Radiance of the Seas at 90,090 GT have nearly identical space ratios.

articles.title: A Port Day That Doesn't Go Sideways: Using the Planner
articles.excerpt: Three failure modes wreck most port days — a too-tight reboarding buffer, a forgotten essential, and a budget that creeps. One printable planner handles all three. Two worked examples: a $169 Cozumel paid-excursion day, a $63 Nassau walking day.

articles.title: Five Nights on Radiance of the Seas: A Bahamas Logbook
articles.excerpt: A 5-night Bahamas trip aboard Radiance of the Seas from Port Everglades — Nassau, CocoCay, sea days. The crew who mattered (Jay in the MDR, Rother in Chops, Jelly at Izumi), Samba Grill as the standout meal, the ship at twenty-four, and an accessibility note grounded in sailing with a family member post-knee-replacement.

articles.title: Ten Days on Quantum of the Seas: A Pacific Coastal Logbook
articles.excerpt: A back-to-back logbook from Los Angeles. Two days at Cabo (whales, El Arco, Lover's Beach), one at Ensenada (North Star at port), the turnaround, and a second pass that ended with a male lion watching me through a diamond grate at Paipai Zoo Safari Park. Day-by-day notes, written honestly.

articles.title: What a Cruise Actually Costs in 2026: Building a Real Budget
articles.excerpt: The advertised fare is typically 40-45% of what a cruise actually costs. Three worked examples through the Budget Calculator — a 3-night Bahamas, a 7-night Western Caribbean, and a 12-night Mediterranean — show the real per-person-per-night math (between $417 and $531 across all three).

articles.title: Hurricane Season 2026 for Cruisers: What El Niño Actually Changes
articles.excerpt: Colorado State University's April forecast calls for a below-average Atlantic season (13 named, 6 hurricanes, 2 major). NOAA's official outlook releases May 21. What it actually means for Caribbean and Bahamas cruisers, what the cruise lines do when weather forces changes, and how to plan around it.

articles.title: Stateroom Sanity Check: The Cabin Mistakes Nobody Warns You About
articles.excerpt: Most cruise cabins are fine. The handful that aren't get assigned to people who didn't know what to look for — under pool decks, above theaters, near galleys, beside elevator banks. The Stateroom Sanity Check tool uses ship-specific exception data from real cruiser reports to catch them before you sail.

articles.title: Is the Drink Package Worth It? Using the Calculator to Find Your Real Break-Even
articles.excerpt: The '5 to 7 drinks per day' rule everyone repeats is wrong for the Royal Caribbean Deluxe package in 2026. Three worked examples through the In the Wake calculator — light, moderate, heavy — showing where a la carte still wins and where Deluxe actually pays off.

articles.title: Hantavirus on Cruise Ships 2026: What the MV Hondius Outbreak Actually Means
articles.excerpt: The MV Hondius Andes hantavirus outbreak in plain numbers: 9 confirmed cases, 3 deaths, one expedition ship out of Ushuaia. What it means for mainstream cruises (nothing) and expedition cruises (real, bounded). WHO, ECDC, CDC sourced.

articles.title: The Royal Beach Club Collection in 2026: What's Open, What's Coming, What It Changes
articles.excerpt: Paradise Island opened in December. Santorini opened in April. Cozumel opens at the end of the year. Royal Caribbean Group is going from two land-based destinations to eight by 2028 — what that means for itinerary planning.

articles.title: Mesoamerican Reef Diving from Costa Maya: A Cruiser's Guide
articles.excerpt: The world's second-largest barrier reef sits less than half a mile from a sleepy Mexican fishing town that happens to have a cruise port next to it. Five local sites, the Mama Viña wreck, and Banco Chinchorro for the advanced divers willing to go further.

articles.title: South Pacific Cruising in 2026: A First-Timer's Primer with Mystery Island at the Center
articles.excerpt: Australia's South Pacific market more than doubled into 2026. Mystery Island, Port Vila, Noumea, and Lifou anchor the standard 7-to-12 night loop from Sydney and Brisbane. Who sails it and how to plan a first sailing.

articles.title: Cruise Tipping for 2026: What You'll Actually Owe, by Line
articles.excerpt: 2026 cruise tipping by line: $17-$25 per guest per day, plus 18%-20% on bar, spa, specialty, and room service, plus optional cash for porters, butlers, and kids' club. All 15 major lines.

articles.title: Caribbean Cruise Trends for 2026: A First-Timer's Guide
articles.excerpt: What's actually changing in 2026 Caribbean cruising — capacity, ports, ships, shorter trips — verified against CLIA, AAA, and port-authority sources.

articles.title: Cruise Cabin Organization: Making Small Spaces Work
articles.excerpt: Magnetic hooks, over-door organizers, packing cubes, and the small comforts that turn a tight cabin into something livable.

articles.title: Cruise Tech & Photography Guide
articles.excerpt: Honest notes on tech and photography for cruises: waterproof pouches, e-readers, portable power, earbuds, and what actually helps you take better photos at sea.

articles.title: The Cruise Duck Tradition: How to Hide & Find Ducks
articles.excerpt: The hidden-duck tradition cruisers love — how to participate, what ducks to bring, and where to hide them.

articles.title: Solo in the Wake: The Practical Guide to Cruising Alone
articles.excerpt: Ship sizes, dining strategies, safety, solo supplements, and practical wisdom for every kind of solo traveler.

articles.title: Freedom of Your Own Wake
articles.excerpt: On choosing solitude without being alone.

articles.title: Why I Started Solo Cruising
articles.excerpt: From that first solo gangway to a thriving community — freedom found at sea.

articles.title: Top 20 First-Cruise Questions (Answered)
articles.excerpt: A first-person guide to planning, packing, ports, and ship life.

articles.title: Visiting (or Moving to) the United States
articles.excerpt: Warm, honest guidance to enjoy America deeply — scale, culture, dining, money, weather, and the art of one great day.

articles.title: In the Wake of Grief: When Loss Needs Water
articles.excerpt: Finding solace and healing in the steady rhythm of the sea.

articles.title: Accessible Cruising: Five Universal Principles for Disabled Travelers
articles.excerpt: Practical guidance for making cruise travel accessible and enjoyable for disabled travelers.
